title: SPECTROPHOTOMETRIC DETERMINATION OF IRON WITH-3-HYDROXY-I-P-SULPHONATOPHENYI-3-PHENYLTRIAZlNE
author: H. K. L. GUPTA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fe III  forms a bluish black water-soluble complex with 3-hydroxy- \(I\) -p-sulphonatophenyl-3-phenyl­triszine in the pH range of 2.0 to 4.3, with absorption peaks at 520 and 650 m µ  Full colour develop­ment is ensured at r : 15 ratio of iron to the reagent. The system obeys Beer's law in the concentra­tion range of to 20 p.p.m. of iron The molecular extinction coefficient of iron complex at 65o inµ has been determined to be 4.47 x 10 3  Nessler's cylinder sensitivity has been found to be  \(I\)  part of iron in 5, 000,000 parts of solution and spot plate identification limit as  \(0.I\gamma\)  iron in o.15 c c of solution.
steps_raw: Fe III  forms a bluish black water-soluble complex with 3-hydroxy- \(I\) -p-sulphonatophenyl-3-phenyl­triszine in the pH range of 2.0 to 4.3, with absorption peaks at 520 and 650 m µ  Full colour develop­ment is ensured at r : 15 ratio of iron to the reagent. The system obeys Beer's law in the concentra­tion range of to 20 p.p.m. of iron The molecular extinction coefficient of iron complex at 65o inµ has been determined to be 4.47 x 10 3  Nessler's cylinder sensitivity has been found to be  \(I\)  part of iron in 5, 000,000 parts of solution and spot plate identification limit as  \(0.I\gamma\)  iron in o.15 c c of solution.
